heroHeadline: STOLTENBERG: 'NATO NOT WRITTEN IN STONE' — Trump Declares 'Will Not Support Us in Future'; Europe Forms Autonomous Hormuz Coalition; Magyar Nominated as Hungarian PM
heroSubtitle: Former NATO Secretary General Stoltenberg warned April 15 that 'it is not a natural law that NATO will exist for the next ten years' after Trump escalated to declaring NATO 'will not support us in the future.' Europe responded by finalizing a 40+ nation Hormuz security coalition (April 17) — explicitly without US participation — as the Wall Street Journal revealed European capitals are drafting contingency plans for NATO operations without US support. NATO Secretary General Rutte completed a European diplomatic circuit (Berlin UDCG April 15, Prague April 16) to reinforce cohesion. Hungary's President Sulyok formally nominated Péter Magyar as PM on April 15, removing the alliance's key internal blocker ahead of the Helsingborg Foreign Ministers summit (May 21–22). The bipartisan US Senate NATO Observer Group pushed back on Trump's withdrawal rhetoric — but has no enforcement mechanism. The alliance remains formally intact and in deepest existential crisis.
footerNote: Data sourced from NATO HQ, White House, US Department of Defense, Reuters, Bloomberg, Wall Street Journal, The Economist, The Telegraph, TIME, Politico, German Federal Foreign Office, Axios, NPR, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, EU News, Swedish MFA, France 24, Euronews, CNN, PBS NewsHour, Gulf News, US Senate Foreign Relations Committee
